steps.title: Bracket the intervening phrase
steps.content: The relative clause begins with "that" and has the verb "aim." The phrase "through community involvement and scientific study" interrupts that clause before the verb and is closed by a comma before "aim."

steps.title: Open with a matching comma
steps.content: A opens the interruption with a dash but closes it with a comma, creating mismatched punctuation. B uses a semicolon after a relative pronoun, and C uses a colon after it; neither is grammatical. A comma after "that" correctly opens the interruption. The answer is D.
